hypothesis: If a Transformer encoder–decoder model is pretrained on large simulated molecular‑dynamics IR spectra and fine‑tuned on real NIST spectra while using patch‑based spectrum embedding, a jointly trained spectral reconstruction alignment loss, and multitask concentration training, then it will more accurately predict the molecular components of binary and ternary IR mixtures (including experimental ATR‑IR mixtures), yielding higher Top‑k accuracy and Tanimoto similarity than models lacking these design elements.
